title: Spin Echo with Whole-Echo Acquisition
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3jit","text":"Purpose","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7unu","text":"Acquisition of a Hahn-echo spectrum with whole-echo acquisition from a quadrupolar nucleus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fg0ie","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8b10","text":"Scope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2it2","text":"This protocol is part of a standard array of experiments to be run on a new sample. It can also be used as preparation for MQMAS experiments, particularly those involving split-t1 acquisition. This protocol can also be used when there is a significant probe or rotor background observed in the spectrum. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":177,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":178,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"16c6j","text":"This sequence imposes a T2’ weighting factor on the intensities of the resonances; while typically negligible for short echo periods, this should be considered when using long echo periods. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":24,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":25,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anb2e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsv8e","text":"When used for an integer spin nucleus, a solid echo (90°-90°) must be used instead of a Hahn echo (90°-180°). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ftbtn","text":"User should be familiar with the power limitations and duty cycle of the probe being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bq6gv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"brl58","text":"Expected amount of time SOP will use: 30 minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"79vp6","text":"Definitions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5k4bq","text":"MQMAS: Multiple-quantum magic angle spinning","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ha0","text":"MAS: Magic angle spinning","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dpvl1","text":"CT: Central Transition","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fnko","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kraj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set pulse p1 and power plw1 to a central-transition-selective 90° pulse. If acquiring data on a spin-1/2 nucleus, ignore all references to central-transition selective and use a 90° pulse.
Set cnst2 to 2 to produce 180° CT-selective echo pulse, or to 1 to produce a 90° CT-selective echo pulse. If the observed nucleus is integer-spin, cnst2 must be set to 1.
If collecting a MAS spectrum, set cnst31 to the MAS rate. If collecting a static, i.e., not spinning, spectrum, set cnst31 to 1e6.
Set the recycle delay , d1 , to 1.3 × T 1 if optimizing for sensitivity, or 5 × T 1 if quantitative intensities are desired. Recall that the intensities are modulated by a T 2 ’ weighting factor.
Set the rotor period loop counter, l1 , to an initial value of 1.
Set ns to a multiple of 16.
Process the spectrum according to procedure for full-echo acquisition.
This involves setting a large first-order phase correction phc1 .
Apodization can be applied using Gaussian, sine bell, or squared sine bell functions.

If half-echo acquisition is desired, left-shift the spectrum until the echo top is the beginning of the FID. With half-echo acquisition the large first-order phase correction phc1 is not required and exponential apodization can be used.
Increase l1 if necessary to either remove background signals or to prevent residual FID from the excitation pulse.
If testing for MQMAS with split- t 1 acquisition, increase l1 until residual FID is no longer overlaps with the whole echo.
If removing background signal from the spectrum, increase l1 until the background is no longer observed.
If the sample is static, set l1 to the desired echo delay in microseconds.
Increase the number of scans, ns , to a value which will provide adequate signal-to-noise while respecting the phase cycle.
Set the pre-acquisition delay, d7 , to 1 μ. This initial value will ensure that the whole echo is acquired, but might allow for residual FID to bleed through for small values of l1 .
creator: NMRFAM Facility
